review_text: Looks nothing like the photos.not really catered towards tents more for camper vans The only redemption was the toilet blocks they were really clean . Also too many rules everywhere .
Stayed 3 nights, quite camp, albeit close to the road, friendly staff, amenities were spotless, no river due to drought, 20 minutes from Girraween National Park for spectacular walks
Have just  returned from 4 nights at this park. Immaculate facilities and park. Nice spacious sites. Hosts Ben & Narelle were fantastic. Will be back again.

Such clean facilities,  very friendly and welcoming environment.  Went with a family group camping and had a great time . Took my doggy Bronson and he loved it there , dog off lead playground was awesome to have .
Communal fire at night was fun
Highly recommended place to stay .

The hosts, Narelle and Ben, are very friendly and accomodating. The property is suffering from a long period without rain but this does not distract from the camping experience. We had a great stay, Thnak You!! PS beware of the trees, some tend to jump out in front of your caravan to commit suicide.

A great location. Ben and Narelle were great hosts explained and gave suggestions on sites around Stanthorpe to visit. Had park get together around the park fire pit most nights. Ben was fantastic with a problem I had with the van and was able to source someone to repair it. Would fully recommend a visit to park. Great place great atmosphere
This caravan park an absolute gem, the owners Ben and Narelle are the most amazing hosts. The sites are big, amenities are spotless and the camp kitchen at another level, has everything you need.

Quite and friendly caravan park. Toilets and showers are clean and well maintained. $1 for 6 minute shower- great water pressure and nice and hot. River was low because of current drought but made it easier to explore 🦆🪾🏕️

The caravan park is in a pretty spot along the Severn River, about 5mins south of Stanthorpe and close to many wineries. Facilities were kept clean and the bins emptied every day. Level drive-through sites with space. Staff friendly and helpful.

We loved our stay here, great location right opposite Winery and short trip into town with plenty to explore in this amazing granite belt. Beautifully clean facilities and hosts Ben and Narelle were fantastic. Being pet friendly, the off leash area for our dog to exercise was a plus. Would highly recommend and will certainly return. Thanks guys!
Great few days away lovely setting and amazing hosts thoroughly enjoyed the evening communal fire pit hosted by the team. Lots of banter and stories. Great clean facilities. Highly recommend 👍

The caravan spaces are roomy and the off grid area near the river provides lovely views with lots of wildlife.  The river was low when we visited due to lack of rain but it still had water and ensured all manner of birdlife right on your doorstep.||The showers were a bit hit and miss and are coin operated due to water restrictions however the facilities are very clean and there is a well equipped camp kitchen.||Kominos winery is directly across the road and have great wine and lovely cheese platters if you are looking for a memorable lunch spot to while away the afternoon.

Great location and lovely park. The whole area was very dry when we visited which does have an impact but lots to do in the area. Ben and Narelle were excellent hosts and we'll definitely be back again. Really enjoyed our stay.
Lovely hosts.  Pity the was not much water in the weir but we enjoyed the outlook and the bird life.  Would highly recommend

Very clean park, quite at night.
Park owners/Manager’s were very polite and friendly always up for a chat.
Would recommend this park for an overnight stay or longer
A nice place to stay

We booked this campground months in advance & had booked a riverside caravan unpowered sites, but as the Stanthorpe area hasn't had any rain since November, the river had dried up to a couple of puddles, it was still a nice sites & the camo hosts were great, we would definitely go back

Great Location, Friendly owners, Clean Ammenties! What more could you ask for!

We have just spent a week at this lovely park on an unpowered riverside site and booked our next stay before leaving (which should tell you something).

Sites:
I typically don't like staying in caravan parks because they tend to be small sites on top of each other but these sites are large and spaced out so you get your own space from other guests and I was so pleased.

Owners: From the moment we arrived the owners could not have been friendlier. They work hard to present the park well for their guests and try to cater for everyone (cabins, tents and vans etc). I'm going to miss our afternoon chats next week when back at work haha.

There are farm animals the kids can feed and an off leash dog area which is a fantastic idea.

Location: Perfect location with easy access to wineries and my fav cafe Jamworks.

River:Yes the River is very low due to the drought (if you look around the area you can tell it's been awhile since they've had rain - I think a local wine maker said they've had no rain since November!) but that just means you get the opportunity to fossick in the river bed for Topaz and Quartz. My screen obsessed son had hours of fun doing this.

Storm King Dam is only about 15 minutes away if you want to kayak and fish meaning you can have the best of both worlds.

Fires: Presently you can't have open fires around the park which when you look at how dry everything is and how little water is the only smart thing to do.

But wait, you can still bring along those marshmallows and toast them on the communal fire!

The owners lit the communal fire each night we were there for everyone to enjoy while still being able to keep an eye on it so as not to start a grass fire (which is super easy to happen when this dry and left unsupervised).

Overall we had a lovely week and am already looking forward to our next trip.

Quick clarification after the owner’s response: Local businesses told us the water was “drained” to support nearby capsicum farms. We understand and respect the park’s decisions, which is evident in my review (including the no-fire policy) — our only concern was not being given a heads-up before arrival so we could prepare or adjust our plans, regardless of any cancellation fees. Also, after reading several low reviews, it appears the owner often focuses on finding issues with their guests rather than taking on the feedback, which I found telling.

No water in the creek & no fires made this a mixed stay.
The good: the camp kitchen is good, the showers/toilets were clean and plentiful, and the showers are $1 for 5 minutes which is very generous (no issues with this at all). The dog park is also a big plus. There is a nice winery across the road. We also appreciated that the tent allocations felt quite spaced out and not overcrowded. It didn’t feel like they were trying to overbook just to maximise numbers.
No water in the creek & no fires (and why it matters): when we arrived, the creek/river was completely dry. It was made out to be that it had just dried up naturally, but later we heard from locals that the water had actually been diverted to support farms during low rainfall. I completely understand that’s out of the park’s control, but we really wish we’d been told prior to arrival, as the water was one of the main reasons we booked.
Same with fires: the place is clearly very dry, so a no-fire policy is completely fair. However, this appeared to be a park-specific rule rather than a state-enforced restriction, and again, a heads-up would’ve helped. We came camping expecting a creek and a fire, and ended up with neither.
Biggest issue: we were placed near the sewage treatment/tank area and unfortunately it stunk, plus the tank noise kicked on and off through the night and kept waking us up.
This park is probably better suited to caravans than tent camping (at least based on where we were put). If you do stay, ask to be camped well away from the sewage area. Also note it’s close to the highway, so expect road noise.

Location was great close to everything a family wants to see.|The manager was super friendly and very helpful, he made our gbabies first camping trip easy.|Well worth the money.|Bathrooms and grounds kept very clean and clear of littler.|Noise level was typical of a camp ground but nothing loud after 2100.

We (4 vans) spent the Easter long weekend camping on the riverfront sites. The park is in a a great location close to many wineries and attractions to explore. Unfortunately the river was almost bare due to no rain however this allowed us to explore the riverbed. Owners Ben and Narelle are very friendly and accommodating, the park clean and tidy and the amenities block clean and only a short walk. Showers are $1 for 5 minutes with lovely hot water and good pressure. There is a communal fire pit at the top of the park and a big chess board. We thoroughly enjoyed our stay and rebooked again for next year.

We were greeted warmly by Narelle and Ben. Our site was big and well maintained. Had the most relaxing time. This place has a real country atmosphere, open fires and farm animals. Awesome sun sets. Dog friendly and big dog park.
Can highly recommend recommended this place.
I stayd here during two month, the owners is incredible people. Thaank you for this safe place
We were very happy to stay here last weekend. This place is very clean and comfortable. We will look forward to coming back again

This trip was both my wife & my first visit to Stanthorpe.|Thoroughly enjoyed the visit, a definite great experience visiting wineries, orchards and strawberry farm. definitely looking forward to our next visit. Accommodation was very clean, pet friendly cabin with A/C and convenient location a short drive into town & local places of interest. Hosts, Ben & Narelle were very friendly & easy to deal with.
Ben and Narelle couldn't do enough for us. Really nice place to camp and relax
Down by the river. Whilst its not flowing at the moment, it was still a lovely retreat.|We will be back.

The owners are fantastic, always there if you require anything.
Unfortunately, when we were there it was in drought conditions but given this we had a fantastic stay. The river had dried up but this opened up different things to explore like fossicking for gems and the like.
Would definitely stay again.

Had our caravan on a powered site.
Very friendly owners kept the park very neat and tidy

We liked this park. Amenities were clean and the park is kept very well. We will be back and recomend this park as it is central to ballandean and stanthorpe.
The pet friendly adpect and dog off leash area was a bonus.

Recently did a long weekend in a studio cabin, the place was beautiful peaceful all amenities were spotless. The onsite managers  were very hands on and approachable at all times. Great little dog friendly location.

Enjoyed our caravan site close to amenities. Also really enjoyed standing around the fire pit that you started up.
The very clean bbq was a joy to use. And the hospitality and friendliness from the owners was great. We had a very enjoyable stay. Cheers
Great stay. Unit lovely and clean. Great that my dog could come and stay also.

We absolutely loved our camping experience on this very beautiful, large open space property.
We stayed on site 30 which was powered and water for extra comfort and convenience. We celebrated our Wedding Anniversary and decided to continue the celebrations over the weekend. Lamb roast whilst dining under the stars near the very large gum tree (torch lit) thanks to hubby was just perfect.
The animal feeding was also lovely for us to enjoy.
We will definitely be returning in the not too distant future.

Thank you Ben and Narelle.
We had a great last minute trip to Stanthorpe and this quiet caravan park was just what we wanted.

This place was absolutely fantastic. We stayed in a great cabin with our fur baby Archie. Dog off leash area as well. The cabin was spacious, exceptionally clean and being only a short drive from town. Quiet and peaceful . We have rebooked for another stay.

Nice quiet spot just out of town.
Clean amenities,  great people who were very helpful and knowledgeable of the Granite Belt area. Definitely be staying again.
Absolutely loved our stay friendly and helpful staff

We had a fantastic stay at this caravan park! The sites were incredibly spacious — plenty of room for our motorhome and dolly without feeling cramped, which made setting up stress-free. The park was well maintained, quiet and very welcoming.

One of the highlights for us was the off-leash dog park — our dogs absolutely loved having space to run and play safely, and it made the stay so much more enjoyable for the whole family.  We enjoyed the lovely goats ad well .

The managers  were friendly and helpful, and the overall atmosphere was relaxed and comfortable. We’d definitely stay again and highly recommend this park to anyone travelling with a motorhome — especially if you’re bringing your dogs along.”
We will be back and highly recommend for caravans and motorhomes and pets . Jenny and Jack .

Wow, what a hidden gem just out of Stanthorpe on the river. Great spot for an overnight. Ben could not have been more welcoming and helpful. Lovely and quiet and a beautiful river with lots of birdlife.
A fantastic little caravan park warm welcome spotless ablution block and a camp kitchen second to non.  Cannot recommend enough

Booked last minute on hipcamp and arrived and went straight to the campsite following instructions provided, management then stormed down on their little buggy very rudely demanding ‘ can we help you? you better get to reception now’. Put us off instantly and we left. After reading other reviews seems to be a pattern - maybe get better care takers if you want your business to be succesful. Photos online are all edited to buggery, awful spot very dirty and not very taken care of to match the managements dirty attitude.

The owners/ care takers that approached us on their golf cart were extremely rude. Not sure whether its a race thing or whatever. We had booked via hipcamp but after that first uncalled for interaction we just left. Location is a catfish not what advertised.

Ben and Narelle are great hosts very nice people
Thanks for the stay guys from Jules and Jeff
Cityncountry 👍😁👍

From the moment we arrived we were made to feel welcome as we were guided down to our riverside un powered site. Great location, great people, loved the goats and the dog park. Even had a beer with Ben and his Mrs (owners) down by the waters edge. We will be back!

We recently stayed on a powered site. The park is well maintained. We enjoyed seeing the goats and sheep everyday. A lovely setting on the water, can only imagine how it looks after much needed rain. Perfect if you are travelling with a dog with a great off leash area. We look forward to visiting again.

A great caravan park with very warm welcome and helpfulness.
The facilities were spotless and more hit water than needed for a shower. Thankyou would love to come again. Fingers crossed you get rain soon Trixie and Harold Hall
Ruhiger Campingplatz, saubere Anlage, schöne Tierbeobachtungen möglich

What a cracking stay over the Australia Day weekend!

There was a mixture of powered sites closer to the highway, the unpowered sites near the dam.

We were on the corner close to the shower blocks, so be aware at night there can be a wait and bring $1 coins for the shower.

There is a couple of spots inbetween the camp spots on the dam/creek side you can walk down and take a look. If you have solar or an EV for power these would be the ideal locations.

Our only issue was our spot had almost no grass, so the granite and sand was a nightmare to get pegs in.

There is a few gas barbys at the main kitchen and a stove top. I only like cooking with wood and charcoal, so I’m not sure if you can cook at your own site. We used a small gas cooker do this weekend as it was bloody hot!

Had a great few days with friends at the Country Style Holiday Park. Good location, facilites are well maintained, and they have a nice communal fire pit. Would definitely stay again on our next trip to Stanthorpe.
Great little park, close to Stanthorpe, amenities clean and tidy, friendly staff and good size caravan sites. Good value.
Well kept, great amenities, nice next to water. Well designed and maintained sites.

​Disappointing experience with the onsite management. Despite booking five sites together well in advance, our group was split up without notice. When the manager was politely asked about the error later that weekend, he was incredibly rude, sarcastic and unprofessional. His attitude towards us turned a fun trip into a sour experience.

The physical layout of this park is quite good; the location is convenient and the drive-through powered sites make setup effortless. However, our experience was significantly marred by the management's attitude. When we attempted to raise a concern, we were met with a dismissive and unprofessional response, being told to only return when we had a "real problem." It is disappointing to see such a well-maintained site let down by a lack of basic customer service and respect for guests.
Simply a wonderful experience. Highly recommend. Kids loved interacting with the goats, rooms clean and comfortable.

This is a lovely park to stay. Location is good, owners are friendly, the park is always clean and tidy. It's not too crowded and the water views are gorgeous.
It's just as nice in summer or winter

What a gem! We stayed two nights in the ‘bush camping’ area (unpowered) and loved it. The river was a few steps down and just gorgeous with heaps of bird live to watch. You can swim, however because it hasn’t rained for a while, I won’t recommend.
The amenities were a short walk away, very clean with $1 coin showers, which we didn’t mind at all as it is very water vise in my opinion.
They also have goats and sheep, which was such a fun to watch the two baby goats play with each other and being silly.
The whole campground is set in a beautiful landscaped property and very well maintained. You can have your own fire in the fire pits on the river bank side, however due to fire restrictions couldn’t, but had a great evening at the communal fire pit with the owners and other campers.
There’s also powered sites and cabins.
One other thing worth to mention is the location of the campground,  there’s plenty of wineries, breweries and distilleries around, however there’s a winery across the road if you don’t want to drive 🍷 🤩
A man made pyramid near by is a little attraction, so is the Ballandean Pub and Motel with a great beer garden and live music on weekends. Newly renovated it’s a must. (Cannot comment on food as we only had drinks)
The owners of Country Style Caravan Park are such nice people, really caring about the grounds and people and so welcoming.
Thank you so much for your hospitality 💕, we will be back!

Beautiful, clean, friendly, quiet and very welcoming caravan park. great location, beautiful river view (with options to swim and paddle board) our kids loved helping out with the goats too. Thank you Narelle & Ben for such a welcoming experience and we look forward to seeing you soon 😊

Stayed 4 nights. Grounds and facilities are some of the most well maintained and clean I have come across. The river is beautiful and calm, great for paddle boarding or kayaking. Close to Stanthorpe for cafes or supplies. Definitely recommend staying here.

Beautiful area to camp (we were in a tent), and the only downside is the occasional sound of vehicles travelling on the highway. Will be very happy to come back here in the future!

Well-kept facilities (seriously, they keep these bathrooms clean and tidy) and grounds. I see the staff out and about a lot every day doing all kinds of care for the grounds,

Friendly staff,

Cash and card available at office
office. They also exchange cash for cash if you need coins for the showers ($1 for 6 minutes fyi),

Office has ice bags and goat food available for purchase at reasonable price. No groceries available for purchase,

Goats and sheep on site in pens (not a petting zoo),

Laundry room facilities: washers and dryers. Coin operated. Can't remember price. Clothesline outside. Has a small stocked bookshelf too,

Communal areas:
Giant chess game board and pieces,
Fire pit and seating,
Camp kitchen equipped with microwave, kettle, toaster, shelving, 2 bbqs, stove,
Fenced dog area with ramp and things for them to do.

15 mins from Stanthorpe shopping area which has kmart hub, cafes,  restaurants,  pharmacy, banks, and heaps of other things.

Fishing: not much! Might be seasonal, and us newbies don't know much about that. But here there have been only a handful of fish visible (while stand up paddle boarding), and hubby has fished twice daily for 3 days and caught nothing.

Water is beautiful though! Not too cold (it is summer now), and have seen children swimming in the river enjoying themselves. Rocky area behind the river dam wall to explore.
We had a great time in country style campsite. Beautiful lake view. Great for Kayaking!! Great for family stay. Very kids friendly.  Thank you for that

Just spent a week at Country Style Holiday Park.
This Caravan Park has great hosts, Ben and Narelle, they are friendly and very helpful.

The park has a relaxed atmosphere, with some sheep and goats to pat and feed, plus a nice walk down to the river within the park. We stayed on the large drive through sites at the top of the park. Great giant chess game to enjoy also.

The  2 Amenity blocks were always clean.

The location is just south of Stanthorpe and close to Wineries and other local sites. Even a winery across the road to enjoy.

We would highly recommend this park and looking forward to our next visit.

Great sites by the river which is good for kayaking, good facilities, close to nice vineyards (incl one straight across the road), giant chessboard which was novel. Goats and sheep. Good cycling conditions for kids. Shall return!
Fox bar Falls it’s a lot better

This holiday park is top shelf. Our hosts Ben and Narelle are absolutely delightful. The whole place is neat and tidy and our cabin was spotless. The amenities were clean and the pet friendly atmosphere was second to none. My wife and could not rate this more highly. 10 out of 10. Thankyou for making our stay so special. Mark and Lee Lewis.

We had a lovely stay at Country Style Holiday Park. We were on a powered site which was a great size. The amenities were clean and the grounds lovely and well kept. We travelled with two dogs and the off-leash area was very handy. It was overall a great place to stay.

Absolutely fantastic place to set up camp, right along the river, goats and sheep are friendly and the baby goats are too cute. The site is beautifully maintained and the hosts are so friendly and helpful. The amenities are top notch, clean and well placed. I really cannot recommend highly enough
Stayed at a power site, we were a large group and they gave us sites right next door to each other. Enjoyed our stay there.

River was very disappointing. Lots of weed, fairly stagnant water. Water was pretty muddy close to shore and access was very limited due to van sites. Access to water via the weir was very slippery and not suitable for littlies. Very different to their website photos.

This was a fabulous caravan park with excellent hosts ! Thank you for a wonderful home away from home !
Sites - excellent
Amenities- excellent
Customer care - excellent
Attention to detail - excellent
Campers behaviour- excellent.
We couldn’t fault this place .
Thank you
Leigh and Brett

Wonderful 2 night stay on an unpowered riverside camp site. Bush camping with the security of a private park. Wonderful birds and ducks to watch, spacious grounds, good clean  ammenities (showers $1 for 6 mins) and very friendly hosts who guided us to our site. Would definitely come back!
We spent a wonderful time in Country Style Caravan Park near the dam. The owners are lovely and the place is peaceful. I recommend

We absolutely loved our stay at Country Style! It was such a pleasure chatting with the owners—they were incredibly friendly, welcoming, and couldn’t do enough for us. They went above and beyond to accommodate our travel plans and make everything work perfectly for us over the Christmas period. Highly recommend!
Camping unpowered was fantastic. The view of the river was great and the staff were so helpful and friendly. We had a great time. Thanks.

We had a quick one night stop over with our caravan on a powered site. The owners are super friendly and gave us a warm welcome, the amenities are clean. The location on the river makes for a beautiful sunset and cool down in the water. We will definitely return if passing through again!

We spent five beautiful nights camped beside the river. Lovely spot; clean amenities; well equiped and clean camp kitchen. Ben and Narellde are friendly and super helpful. Wd have been before and will go again … one of our favourite spots.

We stayed on a large, grassy drive through powered site. The park is well maintained. We had lovely views and we really enjoyed the peaceful location with birdsong as a bonus. The amenities were clean and tidy. Ben and Narelle were wonderful hosts and very attentive. We really enjoyed the Granite Belt vineyards and Girraween National Park. We highly recommend staying at Country Style Holiday Park.
Stayed here 2 nights not to bad we stayed at the unpowered sites next to the wier which is down the back great spot/walks can swim pet friendly also.

What a great place to spend a few days, if not weeks! The room was spotless and the mattress amazing (Room 2). The dog park is great, better than the council one. I'll definitely come back. Ben is simply a legend. All the facilities were clean and very organized. Thanks, Country Style Holiday Park team. See you next adventure!
Very clean and tidy, only a short drive into town for a look around.

Beautiful little spot, very clean and friendly. Parking spaces large but caution to big vans being towed by big cars, turning space tight if you gun ho it.worth the visit. Showers coin operated but one coin more than enough.

We stayed at The Country Style for 4 days in our caravan. It's a nice quiet park, the 2 sets of amenities are clean. I believe you can swim in the creek down the bottom of the park, it was too cold while we were there. It is very lovely to sit and watch the ducks. The new owners are doing a fantastic job bringing this park back to life. Well done guys. The rates are reasonable, location is great, close to any number of wineries and Stanhope. We enjoyed our stay and will be back to see the next lot of changes at the park.

The park is still fantastic!

The dam is flowing and looks brilliant, and Ben’s customer service is as exceptional as ever.

It’s always our favourite place to stay in the area.

It might not look like much from the road, but once you’re in, the park and facilities are absolutely top-notch.
Absolutely beautiful site down beside the river
Great location, lovely views, very friendly people. Amenities were in excellent condition. Monty loved the dog park
Lovely place. Plenty of space.
We camped here and will definitely camp here again. Narrell is just lovely, she makes you feel at home and is ready to help at any time.
Staff very friendly and accommodating. Amenities always clean. Great location on the Severn river, with the Glen Aplin weir.

We love our stay at this beautiful Country Style Holiday Park.The locations handy to the local market, the Christmas tree farm, the cheese and wine factory also Stanthorpe shopping centre. Our whole weekend was enjoyable and memorable. Ben, the owner very friendly and helpful. Thank you Ben and family for our comfortable stay in your park. We will definately come back in the future

An absolutely beautiful place to stay. We had an unpowered site on the banks of the water and enjoyed the views, serenity and birdlife.The owners and staff are super friendly,  the grounds are well-maintained and the amenities are immaculate. Located close to many wineries and a short drive from the shopping centre. The friendly pet goats ( who live in their own caravan) were lovely.  Low rates was the icing on the cake. Definitely one of the best caravan parks we have stayed in.

Beautiful location alongside the Severn River just south of Stanthorpe, great for a relaxing few days or just a restful overnight stay.
Wonderful camp kitchen and amenities, very friendly hosts, doggy friendly with an off-leash area...   and the enclosure with resident goats/sheep was a fun bonus.

We stayed at Country Style Caravan Park Stanthorpe in our caravan for 12 nts. On a lovely tree shaded site with views of the river. Ben was very helpful & always had a smile & kept the park clean & tidy.,we will definitely go back & stay there again

I was very impressed with the location , the facilities available to us on site were fabulous, many thanks
Dawn and Colin
Great place to relax, good sized sites.
Country feel, laid back. Clean amenities. Off leash dog park a winner for me

We had our bi annual cousins reunion at the camp ground, there was a group of 18 of us. We were in powered sites, non powered sites and cabins, all the experiences were excellent..
The facilities were clean, the grounds were well maintained and the management lovely deal with.
I highly recommend staying here when you visit Stanthorpe.

I tent camped 3 nights for a bushwalking trip to Girraween NP. Grassed sites. Quiet, spacious, great aesthetics. Roomy camp kitchen/ covered dining area. Great for groups. Amenities clean and well situated. Responsive friendly staff.
Great caravan park nice and relaxing spot super dog friendly. Had a great day.

Wow what a beautiful place on the severn river. The bird life and nature here is just superb! We camped by the river for two nights and couldn't be happier with our time at Country Style Holiday Park. Ben is great host and all round great guy. Thanks again.
Clean cabin and great facilities in a beautiful location. Mornings by the lake are the best. Would definitely stay there again.
Ben was really helpful.  Great off lead dog area.  Really nice spot to bush camp.
Had a powered campsite for a night. Quiet location with great view over the river. Ben was very easy to deal with and very accommodating. Good camp kitchen and clean amenities.
Staff are fantastic and amenities were so clean!

We booked a powered caravan site and thoroughly enjoyed our stay here. The sites were very large compared to what you get at some caravan parks, and the lake and goats were an added bonus for my son. Would highly recommend staying here

The park I found to be a great park very well laid out amenities were great, clean and very well presented. Camp kitchen well set out everything you need while you are there. Laundry had plenty of machines at a good price. Sites were great size and very easy to get in and out of. Staff were very friendly and helpful. Can’t praise the park enough if we are ever back in that area will definitely stay there again. Many thanks regards Pat and Yoko
Great spot in between everything ,walks,  pub and Stanthorpe

The location is really good. No light pollution. You can capture the milky way.  Not crowded. The facilities are really clean like the toilet. There’s a river in the site. You will  definitely love this spot. The hosts are really friendly and accommodating.

Very nice caravan park. Only stayed one night driving through. Very friendly staff and level sites. Clean bathrooms and laundry. My only comment is $1 per shower is steep as we are already paying $50 a night. Most caravanners are conscious about saving water so not sure why there is a charge. But other than that a great little park.

We spent 2 nights on a powered caravan site. Owners Ben and Narelle are very friendly, helpful and welcoming. Prices affordable. Site was lovely spot, amenities clean with good lighting at night. Definitely will return.

Beautiful park right on the river and a perfect location to explore Stanthorpe and the surrounding area. Nice clean amenities and large drive through sites in the powered section, with unpowered sites down by the river. Such a peaceful place with very welcoming hosts. Highly recommend
Excellent camping at this very well run and maintained park. Nice and quiet, great showers and helpful owners.

We had a fantastic time at Country Style Holiday Park over the weekend. The rooms were fresh, spotless, and exceeded our expectations — such a pleasant surprise! The location was perfect, offering easy access to nearby attractions while still feeling peaceful and relaxed.

The park itself is compact but cleverly designed, with plenty of space to unwind without feeling crowded. One of the highlights for our kids was feeding the goats — they absolutely loved it and it added a lovely touch to the whole experience.

Highly recommend for families looking for a clean, well-located, and welcoming place to stay. We’ll definitely be back!

8 minutes out of Stanthorpe in a lovely rural setting on the river with a weir.
Lovely cabins, warm, clean & comfortable beds.
Great dog park & camp fire pit near cabins.
Really nice stay! Thank you for everything!

How many times have you driven past this gem. What a pleasure it was to camp here. Non powered site by the river. Awesome. It's peaceful and beautiful.  Plenty of birdlife as well.
Amenties have been refurbished. Showers cost $1 for 6 minutes but worth it. Water is  hot with great pressure and  I never ran out of time.
Camp kitchen is well equipped.
There are other camping options as well. Cabins, bush camping and powered sites. Non powered by the river are best.
A great place to stop if your visiting wineries. Stanthorpe is only a short drive up the rd.
Ben and Narelle are very friendly and are doing a great job.
Ben and Narelle amazing owners of this park, it was just beautiful. Always clean and very helpful to anyone, oh do not forget the goats and sheep
Awesome place to stay , the cabins are a great size and everything you need !

We had an awesome long weekend, caravan sites are a big size and level, water pressure was great and the owners are fabulous people. They keep the park and the utilities in pristine condition and we will definitely go there again. They even persevered to win over our furbaby George the chihuahua for a cuddle. Highly recommend this park.

CAMPERS, don't bother!  You're a second-class citizen here.

If you're an RV, then you'll be happy as you'll be hogging the view of the advertised river frontage for anyone else behind you.
Campers, don't even bother.  There absolutely no level camping sites, and you'll be lucky not to end up on gravel or any one of the many ant nests featuring some huge ants up to 2cm long with nasty pincers.

It used to be a lovely place with campers able to squeeze in anywhere that was empty, but the current delightfully customer-focussed owner, Narelle, has not considered equity or reasonable expectations of visitors in her planning the site.

The foreshore is mapped out into areas and, despite the fact there might be little spaces where one could expect to place a chair to view the sunset, you will be asked to "move on" because people are paying "Top Dollar" for those front foreshore sites.
Top dollar turns out to be $4 more than a campsite, so go figure.
A reasonable operator would allocate half of the foreshore for tent campers to create a more equitable situation.

Given that the advertising includes "pristine foreshore", this is essentially false advertising because only the lucky few up front can even access the foreshore, let alone have a view of it!

All we could see was large metal objects and 4WDs from our ant-infested campsite.

One poor camper was asked to remove his four family kayaks from the dam's edge because "it belongs to someone who's paid for that area"....so if you have watercraft you'll be expected to lug it up and down to your campsite, if you're not booked on a foreshore site.

Additionally, Narelle is possibly the rudest campsite operator we have come across.  My Italian academic visitor whom I invited to experience the delights of camping  by the river was aghast at the way I was treated by Narelle when I was simply asking her some questions about who could and couldn't access the foreshore.
Her reply to any feedback is "If you don't like it, go somewhere else", but what she fails to understand is that this is "our holiday", our experience and we have paid money on the expectation that we would be having an experience of the foreshore.

True, we can have 4 square metres on a tiny bit of land juting into the lake, and way, way over to one side of the campground, there's an area we can park ourselves for a casual sit and sip of our cocktails at sunset.

We had moved our tiny table and two chairs in between two foreshore sites to enjoy our breakfast, but this was a NO-NO.

Very un-Australian to block off foreshore to people.  We don't do this with our beaches, so why are we now denied access to one of the features which constitute the main thrust of their advertising?

Because this operator, Narelle, doesn't realise that equity is something that Australians value.

Large RV's and Caravans blocking out the entire view of the lake should not be a thing.

Place them 20 metres behind, up the slope where we campers are expected to be.  They'll still have a view over the heads of the campers and their tents.

Environmentally, their large heavy vehicles cannot be a good thing for the foreshore, especially during wet periods where I have seen it looking really ugly with great divets caused by large wheels and heavy vehicles.

We have just completed a stay at Country Style Holiday Park near Stanthorpe. So good, we stayed 6 nights on a large powered site with river views. Highlights....kayaking and fishing in the river almost at our doorstep. The owners were friendly and welcoming, and were more than happy to direct us to other attractions in the area. We'll definitely be heading back!

The place where you wanna come back. Relaxing place to spend time with family and friends with opportunity to visit granite belt wonders, Girraween and Sundown national parks, wineries and many other attractions. Country style hosts Ben and Narelle are welcoming and pleasant to be with. Wide range of accommodation with very clean amenities. Any opportunity we have we do come back and enjoy Country Style Holiday.

We are staying in a non powered site down on the waterfront. We are a self contained caravan unit. Beautiful spot, lovely and picturesque. Sites are a good size.

Absolutely beautiful park to stay at. Had sites on the river couldn’t have picked a better spot. Ben and his staff were amazing from booking through the many booking amendments through to the stay. Amenities were in great condition and always clean. Will definitely be back.
Fantastic to stay here, very welcoming and the perfect spot between Stanthorpe and Girraween National Park. Excellent facilities that were well maintained. Will be staying here again..:)

We stayed 4 nights at the park in a cabin. It was spotlessly clean and super-comfy. We kept warm with the quiet but efficient heater, the log fire, and the thoughtfully supplied electric blanket. They had a reasonable amount of decent cookware and dishes. Another set of towels would have been nice.
Wi-Fi very limited, only 1GB per day, so don’t expect a working holiday or to stream anything.
Very peaceful and scenic location, the roads out front causes some noise but not enough to disturb. Water pressure excellent.
We enjoyed bird watching on the property, especially by the river.
Giraween NP is a short drive away, and of course all the tourist amenities of the granite belt.
Only had one night unpwered site but right on the river. Peaceful and quiet

My family and I had a great weekend camping experience at country style holiday park.  We camped along the river and found the owners soo welcoming.  The facilities were very clean and looked after.  We will be returning in the future.
Great location camping by the river

We have stayed at Country Style Holiday Park on a number of occasions. This park offers the full range of accommodation from off grid camping in tents or caravan/camper down on the banks of the Severn River, powered and un-powered site's as well as various styles of cabin accommodation. I can’t fault this Holiday Park, just outside Stanthorpe, amongst wineries, cheese factories, as well as pick your own strawberries and apples. This park is owned and operated by Ben & Narelle, they are very accommodating as well as the friendliest hosts we have come across in our travels. This park is also pet friendly, our boy loves it there, they also have an off leash dog park so your dogs can run free.
Highly recommend this park as a must do, the BEST park in the region as far as I am concerned.

This is the most stunning and amazing place to camp right on the river front. The owners were super lovely and very helpful. They made us feel so welcomed. Waking up to this gorgeous water view each morning was amazing and the sunsets were equally as amazing. The beautiful bird life was fun and relaxing to watch as was the Rakali, (Aust water rat). We saw a couple of these many times swimming in the water & sitting on the logs. Very cute little creatures. The area also has amazing things to see. Multiple winery's, cheese places and super hiking trails or and lots of beautiful apple blossom trees! The amenities were very clean and tidy and the camp kitchen - fantastic! This was one place that we did not want to leave. We will def be planning our next trip here. 🥰

This was our 3rd stay at the park and once again we were delighted with the amenities and service provided, nothing was too much trouble.

The park itself has all the amenities you require with no queues for such.  The tranquil setting along the Severne river lends itself to absolute relaxation as you take in all that nature has to offer.  The afternoon walks are an absolute delight as you view the sun setting and the local wildlife go about their day.

The park is located close enough to all the attractions and vineyards that  the Granite belt has to offer.  With some of the local tour operators picking you up from the park.

The greatest accolade one could offer is repeat visit to the park and we will definitely be back.

Thanks Ben and Narelle and the team.

Great location well maintained park
Unpowered have water views
Amenities are spotless
Well worth a look
A wonderful spot on the river. Clean amenities and great service even to your campsite.
We stayed in the bush camp area by the river. It was absolutely beautiful and so peaceful. Thoroughly enjoyed it and recommend it to anyone

We stayed on the waterfront caravan site.  It was fantastic, sites are a large size not on top of other campers.  We used the camp kitchen, it is a good size and has all the necessary supplies.
Location is great, everything is within a short drive.  Park facilities were clean, showers were great.

We spent 5 nights at the Country Style Holiday Park on a river front site.
Absolutely beautiful site with amazing bird life.
The Parks amenities were very clean and the Camp Kitchen had everything you would need.
Ben and Narelle have the Park looking so good and the service was friendly and nothing was too much trouble.
We will be return customers.
Great spot to stay... friendly, clean and open.
Lovely little caravan park.  Powered sites flat and large.  Toilets very clean.  Location is very quiet, not far to drive to  Girraween National Park and Wineries.

We had a fantastic relaxing time once again in this amazing camp ground Ben and Narelle are doing amazing job.Congradulations we will be back
Facilities are spotless and we enjoyed a lovely waterfront spot overlooking the ware once again a big thank you
Lovely place. Cannot answer any other questions because didnt experience them but loved the place.
Wonderful caravan park. Great location close to everything and beautiful outlook over the lake. Facilities were always clean and tidy. Would highly recommend.

Country style holiday park is such a great camping spot, have stayed there twice now and loved both experiences. The park and amenities are always clean, the sites are the perfect size, and Ben and Narelle are so kind and helpful would love to stay here again.

we recommend staying here greeted by lovely owners we stay in a cabin very roomy for a family its equipped with everything you'll need nice quite park close to Stanhope to check out local attractions, I loved seeing your lovely doggies

Thank you, Ann & John,
Another great stay at Country Style Caravan Park. Ben and Narelle are great hosts and the facilities are always pristine and clean.  We'll be back. Again.

Absolutely beautiful camp ground, the sites are very spacious.
So quite you can hear the wildlife.
The amenities are clean and well maintained.
The camp kitchen is very clean and has everything you need including a pizza oven.
The staff are friendly and helpful.

Amazing caravan park to stay at, very ,clean, kept grounds friendly staff, pet friendly,  fire pits for hire small cafe with great coffee n toasties,relaxing,plenty of room and amenities. Lovely country outlook
We had a lovely 2 nights stay in the well appointed cabin. The owners were friendly and helpful.

Everything from the booking process through to the onsite experience was wonderful.  The hosts Ben and Narelle were extremely helpful and accommodating and even took the time to share stories around the campfire of their time managing caravan parks.

Great location, large powered sites and friendly staff. We were welcomed by Narelle and Ben and nothing was too much trouble. Just remember to take your $1 coins for hot water showers and your own fire wood for the cold evenings. Highly recommended!
Stayed a few days there in our van. Friendly staff , clean facilities . Great new off leash area for well behaved dogs. Beautiful position on the Severn River.

Spectacular camping site with a beautiful dam. Owners are very friendly and they have kept the place really well maintained. The bathrooms and kitchen area are immaculate. Highly recommend and will definitely be back!

What an awesome spot in Cold Country! The riverside spots are stunning and it’s a great place if you are travelling with dogs. Narelle and Ben made us all feel very welcome and the off leash dog run is fantastic! One of the best little set ups we have encountered in our travel. We will definitely be back.
Friendly and welcoming staff, picturesque riverside location with great amenities. Close to the spectacular Girraween national park and some terrific wineries. Really enjoyed the stay.

Beautifully located,quiet holiday park on the Severn River - perfect for exploring the granite belt region. Hosts are friendly, helpful and the facilities are spotless. We were on a caravan site with views over the river - idyllic!

Stayed recently at Country Style holiday park. great hosts Narelle and Ben, park was clean and lovely hot showers. great camp kitchen with gas, microwave, BBQ, fridges all you could want. communical fire pit, large chess board. Off lead area for the fur babies. maybe a book exchange book case so you can share books with other travellers.
Good people and nice location. Will be back for sure.

We had a great experience this past weekend. Narelle was lovely, even lending me a warm jacket as the temperature really dropped.  Very peaceful to camp, we had a powered site with plenty of room.  It's a dollar a shower but we'll worth it, good water pressure and nice and hot. We will definitely visit again, slightly out of Stanthorpe but surrounded by wineries 🍷 👌

We have stayed twice at Country Style Holiday Park in our caravan. The amenities are top notch, we had friends that stayed in a cabin and they are basic but comfortable. The sites are spacious, power and water is always a plus. The only thing that they don’t have that would be good is a fire pit on every site. The owners are lovely and we had to make a couple of changes with dates etc and they were accommodating. Will stay again.

A beautiful place to stay! We stayed by the water and it was an amazing view. The amenities were super clean. We wish we could have stayed longer. Definitely will be back with friends. 100% reccomend.
What an amazing place! And beautiful atmosfer. First time visited stanthorpe. I met Ben in Country Style Holiday Park. He would be helpful and kind! Thank you Ben! 🇦🇺

Ben and Narelle are fantastic hosts and have done a great job since they took over there. The new firepit was an added bonus this time and we met some lovely people. Room has everything you need (stayed in an ensuite room). Cooking appliances made it better than having to go our and spend lots at restaurants. We will definitely return.
Country Style Holiday Park is extremely welcoming. Our cabin was spotless - 10/10 for cleanliness. Facilities were perfect for our stay. Thank you.
Wonderful hosts. A true  home away from home.

We choose to Bush Camp /Off grid down beside the water.
Each time we visit we notice improvements by the wonderful owners.
Our friends have camped in the units and love them, very clean and warm, even electric blankets on the beds if needed.
There is so much to do in the area and this Holiday home/ park is perfect.
Clean and tidy plenty of room for our van will be back
Ben and Narelle were excellent hosts we had a great stay. Would 100% recommend.

The cabin we stayed in was a pet friendly cabin. Perfect size for us.
Great location. We will be back. And we will camp on the water.

We booked a Queen cabin plus and didn't realise that there was no ensuite. I have stayed here before and thought I booked the same thing which was a 'Unit' but was told otherwise upon arrival. The website doesn't specify and only says ' close to amenities'. We paid $570 for 4 nights for a room with no ensuite and then had to pay for the showers. A big learning for me to triple check and then double check! In saying this, the communal bathroom was cleaner than others I have experienced. The linen and crockery in the room was nice and new and not gross!

Ben & Narelle were our hosts for 4 days. We have one day left, but I know we'll be back. This place has the best vibe.
Nature Lovers paradise in a pristine setting on the Severn River.

Naralle and Ben are the perfect hosts. Highly recommend. We will stay again.
Great campsite, clean toilets nice kitchen, direct at a lake. The owners are super kind and always down for a chat. We stayed there with two cars  and had a really good time

Thank you so much Ben and Narelle we absolutely loved staying at the park and you both are terrific hosts.  Facilities spotless, lovely river camping even got to see the Rakali. Great base for all the surrounding attractions.  We will be back Glen and Sue

Just spent 2 nights at Country Style Holiday Park in a river front site. It was such a beautiful relaxing spot. Ben and Narelle were so welcoming and helpful. Will b back again when we can. So close to everything in the Stanthorpe area.

We spent 5 nights at the park nice and peaceful great amenities and friendly owners who could not do enough to make sure our stay was enjoyable close enough to stanthorp for all your needs I would highly recommend staying here

Courteous, helpful, going over and above in all area's,

Absolute spectacular river camping with great amenities. Lovely staff greet you upon arriving and Ben will show you to your campsite. Words can’t describe the views but pictures paint a thousand words. Great place to unwind and see the sights of the granite belt. The wineries are a must.

We stopped here after a break down - and so glad we did.  What absolutely lovely helpful people.  We had no car - they checked if we had groceries and to let them know if we need anything. We stayed in our caravan - but their cabins looked fresh and the amenities and gardens were so impressive - clean and well kept.  The view down to the river was devine.  We will be back next time we are through - dogs and fires welcome which for us is an added bonus.  You can also walk across (5 min) to the winery where they have the most amazing platters for lunch.

We love our annual visit here. It's a great venue and the location is perfect for us. Very dog friendly (keep on lead) and clean amenities. Ben and Narelle are very accommodating and helpful. Already booked for next year.

We had a lovely time apart from the rain we still got out to enjoy the wineries and local produce. The hospitality was fantastic, we will definitely be back.  Hopefully get a camp spot  near the water.   Kim and Steve
We stayed 5nights over Easter and had a great time. Great park with owners that are friendly and welcoming.  Will definitely be recommending to friends.
Beautiful spot. Well-run. Good facilities.
Great campsites by the river. Helpful team at the desk and good wine across the road. I'll be back

Place is a joke, thought it was family friendly owners presented themselves as Gestapo and where very rude constantly walking past nitpicking at the kids for having fun and for us adults having a few drinks way before 10pm, will never return as the management is a absolute joke, nothing but rude towards our family! Do not recommend if your looking too have a good holiday, find somewhere else
Neat, clean and quiet what more can one say, yes I would recommend the stay as value.
My favourite camping location for this region when exploring Giraween National Park. Request a riverfront site and book early!
Beautifully furnished modern cabins, great location with onsite facilities and lagoon. Very comfortable stay, Ben was absolutely wonderful and accomodating. Will definitely be staying again
Great place to camp next to the river.  No power or water. Beautiful and peaceful.
Great Camping plac,e right on the weir. Lots of birds and facilities were great. Good Place to relax and a lot of things to see in the granite belt
We had a powered caravan site. We thought the o/nite rates were reasonable and the amenities kept clean and tidy. Great location on the Severn River near the Wier. Also not too far from town.
Great camp spot, right near the water, shade and lots of bird life. Could have a fire but too hot unfortunately. So much to see and do in Stanthorpe and surrounding area, will stay again!

Easy to pull in with caravan. Large drive through site meant we didn't need to undo the caravan for one night. Good rate for power and watered site ($41). Even though there's a tiny charge of 20c for a 3 minute shower, this is explained in the park leaflet given at check in. My only whinge would be that the grass could have been mown as the caravan sites looked a bit scraggly.
Great spot.  Very clean amenities and Ben was extremely helpful.  Nice to kayak on Severn River. Beautiful birds.  Will be back
Facilities dirty, she is not working not working, not family friendly, got told to go sit down by playing game of cricket, reception very rude overcharged and not worth your money
Ben and Narelle are the perfect hosts.  Couldn’t be more accommodating when booking late for the Australia Day weekend.  Amenities are very clean and the park well maintained.

Awesome park which has been under new ownership for past 18 months or so.
Park is well presented with large drive through powered sites or beautiful river front unpowered sites. Ammenities are immaculate as was camp kitchen.
Owners friendly and helpful.
Park close to  Stanthorpe or 40 min to Tenterfield.
If you had avoided this park when under previous owners now is the time to give them a try. You will be pleasantly surprised.

Lovely place. Very relaxing and a great base to visit sites in the Granite Belt.
Host (Ben) very organised and friendly. Amenities were the cleanest I've seen in a long while.

Thanks for a great few days.

We stayed here over Christmas for 6 nights with our 2 cats and it was amazing. Was lots to see around Stanthorpe and surrounds and this was a great base. The amenities were clean and it has a laundry. Peaceful and the river was beautiful. Ben and Narelle were lovely. Will definitely be back .
A beautiful spot to stay on the riverfront. So quiet and peaceful. Enjoyed bird watching, sunsets and just being by the water. Clean amenities. Friendly owners.

Lovely, clean camp. Very professional set up by Ben. Would definitely stay again and would recommend to others.

I don’t often give all stars but this place is Gorgeous   Ben was great with our after hours check in. Room was well appointed. For a quick overnight stay. We will include this place for a longer stay next time

Good location. Clean practical cabins. Good facilities.

Wow this Caravan Park was a pleasant surprise. We stayed in the Bush camping section right on the beautiful River. So much bird life it was amazing.
Would Definatley recommend a stay here. Will be back.

Family cabin room is tastefully decorated, clean and has more than enough bits and pieces to get you through a few days of 'camping'. The managers are friendly and helpful and the kids enjoyed biking around the sites and going to the lake for an explore. The website could do with an update, as I found some things hard to find, but the managers were happy to help with any questions I had.

We spent a wonderful week here, Narelle was so attentive, the sites are a nice size, the giant chess was a big hit with our girls and we made full use of the laundry and camp kitchen!
It was the perfect base camp to explore the Granite Belt and sit back and relax, thank you! 🙂

Not to our liking.  Well and truly overpriced for what it is.  Just being able to say you are a Big 4 when you are nowhere near the standard of most Big 4 parks should not justify the rate you charge for what amounts to little more than a dirt patch.  The 1Gb free wi-fi is so pathetic that we just gave up trying to connect.  The ridiculous notion that you don't give refunds because there "is a contract" is laughable. We have been vanning around Oz for over 4 years and have never encountered such rubbish. Even if you have a no refund policy, perhaps consider how you frame a refund refusal rather than just stating continually "you have a contract". I suggest that you use the $38 you wouldn't refund and head to Ballandean Estate to get an opportunity to see how customer service/relations works.  Offering me a customer credit to be used when I come back was totally offensive to me as I won't be back.

This would have been a good park to stay at if it wasn't for their septic system which smelt atrocious depending on the wind direction.  There were a few near misses where my wife and I came close to throwing up and looking around the campsite other guests were in the same predicament.
What a lovely lady Narelle is, warmly welcomed us late after a very long drive. Quote from the fam “they’ve really gone to trouble to make the room feel cosy”

Amenities are always kept spotless
And Narelle and the team always look after you
Super place, the river camp sites have to be seen to be believed!!

Love this caravan park! Plenty of space, beautiful lake, very clean facilities, quiet and the owners really know how to run a park. Highly recommend and can't wait to stay again.
Quiet comfortable and clean camping. Super friendly greeting upon arrival.
Lovely little park with choice off drive through power site or off grid next to the water. Very peaceful and great price.
There is a lot of love spent on this camp ground, l love it too.

Great place to relax. Perfect location. Quite peaceful park

Very friendly helpful people. We will most certainly stay there again.

This is our second visit to Country Style Caravan Park in a powered site. Very helpful staff and amenities are very clean and well looked after. Location is great - in the middle between town and Ballendeen (wineries). Close to Girraween National Park too.
Only comment is the website alludes to Fire Pits - you need to bring your own now.  Not a big deal but needs to be updated on website to be clear; they are allowed but you need to bring your own.  Will be back again for sure!
We enjoyed our stay in the cabin. Perfectly clean, and bigger than what was expected. Fire heater is great. We were nice and warm on a freezing winter morning/night. Would book again.
I would like to thanks Bent he caravan park for his friendly service.

We stayed in a powered site and it was very peaceful. The staff at the reception are so welcoming and lovely and the bathrooms are kept very clean as well as the camp kitchen. We have been here numerous times and keep coming back..love this place and the staff are fabulous
Great caravan park just out of Stanthorpe. Friendly owners, nice wide drive thru sites. Clean facilities. Best spots are in the non powered sites near the river.
The rooms were well heated, bedding was warm and the communal fire place was a hit.

Great caravan park with super clean and ample amenities in a beautiful location by the river, as well as being close to Stanthorpe and Girraween National Park. Ben & Narelle are lovely and we will absolutely visit again if passing through the area.
